description: You are a field archeologist compiling an excavation report for Site 42WN301. Format the unformatted document at /home/ga/Documents/excavation_report_42WN301.odt to meet State Historic Preservation Office (SHPO) submission standards. Format the main title 'Excavation Report: Site 42WN301 (Black Creek Ruin)' as bold, centered, and at least 16pt font. Insert a generated Table of Contents after the author block. Apply 'Heading 1' style to the seven main sections (Introduction, Methodology, Stratigraphy & Soils, Feature Descriptions, Artifact Catalog, Radiocarbon Dating Results, Summary & Conclusions) and 'Heading 2' style to the three feature subsections (Feature 1: Hearth, Feature 2: Midden, Feature 3: Post Hole). In the Stratigraphy section, organize soil layers and levels into a nested list. Convert the pipe-separated artifact data into a 7-column table (Catalog #, Unit, Level, Artifact Type, Material, Count, Weight (g)) and the raw radiocarbon data into a 5-column table (Lab Number, Sample Context, Material, Conventional Radiocarbon Age, Calibrated Date (2-sigma)). Set all standard body text to Justified alignment and save the completed document. The document is already open in Calligra Words.

body_alignment_samples: Site 42WN301, commonly known as the Black Creek Ruin
Excavations were conducted using standard archaeological procedures
The site represents a short-term residential base
